content:
Given the undirected dynamic graph with the edges: [(0, 1, 0), (1, 2, 1), (0, 2, 2), (5, 8, 2), (4, 5, 3), (2, 9, 8), (0, 5, 11), (3, 7, 11), (4, 8, 11), (3, 6, 13), (4, 9, 14), (0, 4, 15), (6, 8, 16), (1, 8, 17), (2, 8, 22), (2, 5, 23), (0, 6, 26), (2, 6, 26), (0, 1, 27)], we need to find when node 5 and node 8 are first connected.

We will scan each edge and collect the time t whenever u and v match the two queried nodes (in either order).

1. (0, 1, 0) - Time 0
2. (1, 2, 1) - Time 1
3. (0, 2, 2) - Time 2
4. (5, 8, 2) - Time 2
5. (4, 5, 3) - Time 3
6. (2, 9, 8) - Time 3
7. (0, 5, 11) - Time 4
8. (3, 7, 11) - Time 4
9. (4, 8, 11) - Time 5
10. (3, 6, 13) - Time 5
11. (4, 9, 14) - Time 6
12. (0, 4, 15) - Time 6
13. (6, 8, 16) - Time 7
14. (1, 8, 17) - Time 7
15. (2, 8, 22) - Time 8
16. (2, 5, 23) - Time 9
17. (0, 6, 26) - Time 9
18. (2, 6, 26) - Time 10
19. (0, 1, 27) - Time 10

Now we have collected all the times when nodes 0 and 1, 1 and 2, 2 and 3, 3 and 4, 4 and 5, 5 and 6, 6 and 7, 7 and 8, 8 and 9, 9 and 10, and 10 and 11 are connected.

We will compare the minimum time with the maximum time to find the earliest time when node 5 and node 8 are first connected.

The minimum time is 0, and the maximum time is 10. Therefore, the earliest time when node 5 and node 8 are first connected is 0.

Answer: 0
